checkedEvidence: Screenshot or recording shows the first usable CoreApp screen
No blank or blocked loading surface is visible
Startup health summary is reachable from Settings/About
surfaces.notes: 2026-06-21 packaged CDP capture used the current 2.4.12-beta.8 artifact with isolated userData on port 9433. The first usable main CoreApp screen is the Settings/onboarding surface, not a blank or indefinitely loading page; DOM evidence records version 2.4.12-beta.8, build metadata, and Startup health text in the Settings/About content. Onboarding permission guidance remains visible in the screenshot as a real first-run gate, while startup-health-summary-dom.json proves the Startup health summary is reachable from the same main Settings target.

checkedEvidence: Idle state is visible before query input
Searching or recommendation warm-up state is visible
No-result state shows retry and File Index settings actions
Result source/status/reason pills fit without overlap
surfaces.notes: 2026-06-22 R2D packaged recapture uses the rebuilt 2.4.12-beta.10 signed app bundle, TUFF_STARTUP_BENCHMARK_ONCE=1, and isolated profile /tmp/tuff-corebox-r2-profile-20260622-r2d. The ordinary core-box target is reachable; resize no longer sticks at 720x56 for visible search states. Searching/warm-up is visible at 720x243, and the no-result screenshot covers retry plus File Index settings at 720x195 / 1440x390. 2026-06-22 R2I reused the initialized packaged profile and captured a real result query for screenshot: the ordinary core-box window resized from 720x56 to 720x242, returned 2 rows, and showed source/status/reason pills without overlap, including the system row source badge 系统, status 系统 · System Actions, and reason · System Actions. corebox-search-states is now passed; the global visible gate still remains open for broader pending surfaces.

checkedEvidence: Summary counts are visible
Source filters cover UWP/Store, Steam, shortcuts, protocol, AppRef, and path entries
Diagnostic filters distinguish attention, found, unchecked, and disabled entries
Empty states distinguish no entries from filtered-out entries
surfaces.notes: 2026-06-24 packaged CoreApp 2.4.13-beta.1 capture used apps/core-app/dist/mac-arm64/tuff.app, isolated userData /tmp/tuff-app-index-workbench-20260624060402, and Settings -> File Index -> App Index Manager via CDP. The probe seeds managed entries through the typed appIndex transport where platform validation allows it, then uses isolated SQLite fallback only for UWP and custom protocol samples that macOS validation rejects, so the packaged UI reads real local DB state. Evidence covers 6 visible source samples (UWP/Store, Steam, shortcut, protocol, AppRef, path), diagnostic states (found/已命中, unchecked/未诊断, disabled/已禁用, attention/需要处理), and a Steam + disabled filtered-empty state distinct from an unconfigured app index. app scanner logged spawn EBADF during capture and is recorded in probe failures, but the probe ok remains true because the manager UI evidence and diagnostic JSON gate passed.

checkedEvidence: Browser-open failure keeps the device authorization session alive
Manual login URL copy action is visible
Short user code copy action is visible
Timeout and network failure copy is user-readable
surfaces.notes: 2026-06-24 packaged 2.4.13-beta.1 capture used isolated userData profiles and TUFF_VISIBLE_EVIDENCE_AUTH=1 with TUFF_STARTUP_BENCHMARK_ONCE=1. login-browser-open-failure-probe.json on CDP 9481 forced TUFF_VISIBLE_EVIDENCE_AUTH_BROWSER_OPEN_FAIL=1 and captured the Settings login recovery dialog still waiting with manual login URL copy, short-code copy, reopen, and cancel actions; both copy buttons reported success through the typed ClipboardEvents.write transport. login-timeout-or-network-failure-probe.json on CDP 9482 used TUFF_VISIBLE_EVIDENCE_AUTH_LOGIN_TIMEOUT_MS=3000 and captured readable timeout copy with retry/close actions; the JSON also records the network failure classification copy for review.

checkedEvidence: CoreBox AI Ask text.chat success preview is visible
CoreBox AI Ask clipboard image vision.ocr to text.chat success preview is visible
Text and OCR success previews show a non-empty answer without empty-response copy
Provider, model, latency, trace id, and input kind metadata are visible for text and OCR paths
Quota exhausted failure shows a credits or team quota recovery hint
Model unsupported failure shows a supported model or capability recovery hint
Permission denied failure does not call Intelligence SDK and shows a permission recovery hint
Local/Ollama preferred routing does not call disabled Nexus provider and shows routing trace or provider metadata
Logged-out failure shows a sign-in recovery hint
Provider unavailable failure shows a provider health or settings recovery hint
Copy failure remains visible inside the preview
surfaces.notes: 2026-06-18 packaged app + isolated userData + CDP hotkey capture opened the real CoreBox target; bodyClass=MacIntel core-box and inputIdExists=true. 2026-06-18 provider-enabled AI Ask probe loaded touch-intelligence, enabled touch-intelligence.intelligence-ask search provider, entered a real CoreBox query, and captured visible NEXUS_STREAM_UNSUPPORTED model/capability failure UI. 2026-06-18 runtime permission-denied probe loaded touch-intelligence with grants, revoked intelligence.basic through packaged renderer IPC before CoreBox AI Ask execution, and captured visible permission recovery UI without an Intelligence SDK invocation. 2026-06-20 Local/Ollama routing probe used packaged app + isolated userData + precompiled touch-intelligence widget + local qwen2.5:3b, disabled tuff-nexus-default, and captured visible local-ok answer plus Routing provider metadata: provider local-default, model qwen2.5:3b, latency, trace, input kind text, capability text.chat. 2026-06-21 quota exhausted probe used a fresh Chromium userData profile copied from the seeded Tuff business profile, loaded touch-intelligence from DB, kept touch-intelligence.intelligence-ask enabled, seeded plugin quota disabled, and captured visible AI quota exhausted UI with retry/usage recovery copy; raw app logs also recorded [Intelligence] Quota exceeded: Quota is disabled for this caller. Startup-time missing intelligence.basic blocks plugin enablement before CoreBox AI Ask UI appears; that attempt is a blocker note, not permission-denied UI evidence. Raw probe logs, pid files, output mirrors and duplicate stage screenshots are intentionally not referenced by the manifest. 2026-06-21 logged-out probe used packaged app + isolated userData with no signed-in auth state and captured fail-closed sign-in recovery UI without provider unavailable/model unsupported signals. 2026-06-21 provider-unavailable probe used packaged app + isolated signed-in profile, kept all text.chat providers disabled including tuff-nexus-default, and captured Provider unavailable recovery UI after fixing touch-intelligence error classification for "No enabled providers ... capability not supported". 2026-06-21 text.chat success probe used the same signed-in Local/Ollama profile with tuff-nexus-default disabled and captured a visible non-empty answer plus provider local-default, model qwen2.5:3b, latency, trace, input kind text, and capability text.chat metadata. 2026-06-21 OCR handoff probe used a fresh signed-in Chromium profile derived from the text-success profile, excluded stale DevTools/Singleton locks, synced the installed touch-intelligence plugin entry to the current source hash, and captured visible OCR response, text.chat capability, provider local-default, model qwen2.5:3b, latency, trace, input kind text,image, and image-to-text context copy. The OCR artifacts remain under raw/ because they are accepted AI-STABLE-02 evidence captures, while broader report hygiene still keeps exploratory logs and duplicate stages out of the manifest. 2026-06-21 copy failure packaged evidence closed: CDP 9412 triggered MetaOverlayEvents.action.execute -> CoreBoxEvents.metaOverlay.itemAction -> useActionPanel -> CoreBoxEvents.item.execute against an installed dist/build touch-intelligence plugin with precompiled build.widgets; packaged-ai-ask-copy-failure-probe.json is ok=true and packaged-ai-ask-copy-failure.png shows the answer preview notice with 复制失败, clipboard.write, and 请在插件权限中允许 clipboard.write 后重试。

checkedEvidence: Selected-text context or recovery hint is visible
Translate/summarize/rewrite/explain/review actions are visible as applicable
AI result preview is visible
Copy, replace clipboard, retry, and confirmation states are visible
surfaces.notes: 2026-06-24 packaged CoreApp 2.4.13-beta.1 evidence used an isolated profile (/tmp/tuff-omnipanel-evidence-20260624-9485) and real OmniPanel renderer interaction over CDP. Local/Ollama provider local-default was configured with qwen2.5:3b and Nexus disabled; AI 改写 invoked text.rewrite successfully through provider local, model qwen2.5:3b, trace local-1782279378320-f5jm7e, and latency 1234ms. Captures show selected-text context/recovery hint, five writing actions (AI 翻译, AI 摘要, AI 改写, AI 解释, AI Review), AI result preview metadata chips, Retry/Copy/Replace Clipboard actions, explicit replace confirmation state (待确认 / 确认替换), and no-selection recovery hint.

checkedEvidence: Settings show Assistant enabled and voice wake disabled

Clicking the floating ball opens the Voice Panel beside the ball
surfaces.notes: Packaged 2.4.13-beta.1 probe used isolated userData, enabled Assistant/floating ball with voice wake disabled, dragged the floating ball from 220/220 to 316/292, restarted the packaged app, confirmed persisted position, and clicked the ball to open the Voice Panel beside it.

checkedEvidence: Clipboard image translation starts from the Assistant Voice Panel
The Assistant action reads the current clipboard image instead of initiating screen capture
Translated clipboard image result appears in the image translation pin window or detached widget
Empty clipboard image and provider fallback remain visible and recoverable
surfaces.notes: Packaged 2.4.13-beta.1 probe ran with isolated userData. Success scene uses a narrowly gated visible-evidence clipboard image file under the benchmark userData dir after real clipboard read; empty clipboard removes that file and remains a real IMAGE_UNAVAILABLE path. Provider fallback reruns without the success mock and shows the real provider unavailable recovery hint.

checkedEvidence: Dry-run or execute migration evidence summary is attached
Readiness, blockers, migrated/skipped/failed counts are visible
No provider secret is copied into metadata or evidence text
Registry-primary readiness is not claimed for dry-run-only evidence
surfaces.notes: 2026-06-24 Nexus local-only dry-run evidence used the real /api/dashboard/intelligence/providers/migrate handler, migrateLegacyIntelligenceProvidersToRegistry bridge, MockD1Database, and a seeded OpenAI legacy intelligence provider. The summary records mode dry-run, readiness planning, registryPrimaryReady no, total 1, migrated/skipped/failed counts 0/0/0, blockers migration_dry_run_only and migration_not_executed, and the would_create action with secret=unchanged. The dry-run test asserts no provider registry rows or secure-store credential rows are written and that the evidence summary does not include encrypted-local-secret-placeholder or raw API key material. This closes only the migration-evidence artifact requirement and does not claim registry-primary runtime readiness.
